card.intuition: A lazy heap of heap-ordered trees. Insert and decrease-key are O(1) amortised — work is deferred until extract-min, which consolidates trees so no two roots have the same degree. The Fibonacci sequence appears in the bound on tree degrees.
card.analogy: A messy pile of sorted stacks: you lazily toss new items on top and defer reorganisation until you need the minimum. When you finally dig for the minimum, you reorganise into a clean set of stacks — but you've amortised the cleaning cost over many insertions.
card.time_complexity: O(1) amortised insert, decrease-key, merge; O(log n) amortised extract-min
when_to_use: Theoretically optimal Dijkstra: O(E + V log V) using Fibonacci heap decrease-key
Problems requiring many decrease-key operations relative to extract-min
MST algorithms (Prim's) achieving O(E + V log V)
gotchas: In practice, Fibonacci heaps are rarely used — large constants and pointer complexity outweigh theoretical advantages
Mark bits track whether a node has lost a child since last made a root (for cascading cut)
Consolidation ensures at most O(log n) distinct root degrees — uses Fibonacci number property

exercise.prompt: Implement a simplified Fibonacci heap: insert, find-min, and merge (union) operations.
exercise.starter_code:
class FibNode:
    def __init__(self, key):
        self.key    = key
        self.degree = 0
        self.marked = False
        self.parent = None
        self.child  = None
        self.left   = self
        self.right  = self

class FibHeap:
    def __init__(self):
        self.min_node = None
        self.n        = 0

    def insert(self, key):
        node = FibNode(key)
        self._add_to_root_list(node)
        if self.min_node is None or node.key < self.min_node.key:
            self.min_node = node
        self.n += 1
        return node

    def find_min(self):
        return self.min_node.key if self.min_node else None

    def merge(self, other):
        """Merge two Fibonacci heaps, return combined heap."""
        if not self.min_node:
            return other
        if not other.min_node:
            return self
        # Concatenate root lists
        self_last  = self.min_node.left
        other_last = other.min_node.left
        self_last.right  = other.min_node
        other.min_node.left = self_last
        other_last.right = self.min_node
        self.min_node.left = other_last
        # Update min
        if other.min_node.key < self.min_node.key:
            self.min_node = ___________
        self.n += other.n
        return self

    def _add_to_root_list(self, node):
        if self.min_node is None:
            node.left = node.right = node
        else:
            node.right = self.min_node
            node.left  = self.min_node.left
            self.min_node.left.right = node
            self.min_node.left = node
hints: After merging the root lists, the new min is whichever heap had the smaller minimum — set self.min_node = other.min_node if other's minimum is smaller
exercise.test_code:
h1 = FibHeap()
h1.insert(5); h1.insert(3); h1.insert(8)
assert h1.find_min() == 3

h2 = FibHeap()
h2.insert(1); h2.insert(7)
assert h2.find_min() == 1

merged = h1.merge(h2)
assert merged.find_min() == 1
assert merged.n == 5
print('PASS')
